query_result.reasoning:
Excellent! I now have all the information I need to answer this question comprehensively. Let me also verify the specific dollar impact mentioned in the filing - that changing the average useful life of satellite assets by just one year would affect operating income by approximately $480 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, and $170 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.

Let me also check the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation for the most recent period (Q1 2026) to confirm the structure.

parsed_args.prompt: What is the sensitivity disclosure about changing satellite useful life by one year? What is the dollar impact on operating income for 2025 and Q1 2026? Quote the exact disclosure language. {{spacex_s1}}

tool_output.output:
## Sensitivity Disclosure: Satellite Useful Life

The exact disclosure language from the MD&A/Notes section reads:

> "If the average remaining flights for our flight vehicle and spacecraft had been estimated to be five more or fewer flights, the impact to our operating income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and three months ended March 31, 2026 would not be material. **If the average useful life of our satellite assets had been changed by one year, it would have an approximately $480 million and $170 million impact on our operating income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and three months ended March 31, 2026, respectively.**"

### Dollar Impact Summary

| Period | Impact on Operating Income |
|---|---|
| Year ended December 31, 2025 | ~$480 million |
| Three months ended March 31, 2026 | ~$170 million |

This sensitivity is disclosed alongside the company's critical accounting estimates for property, plant, and equipment, where it notes that satellite useful lives are determined based on factors such as on-orbit performance, orbit-raise timing, expected service capability, and the evolution of constellation density and technology. The estimated useful lives for satellites per the accounting policy table range from **3 to 5 years** (broadband satellites: 5 years; first-generation mobile satellites: 3 years). When useful lives are revised, the company adjusts the depreciation rate prospectively to reflect the revised estimates.

| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Income (loss) from operations | $(662) | $1,188 | $(2,469) | $(1,943) |
| Add: | | | | |
| Depreciation and amortization | 166 | 783 | 1,493 | 2,442 |
| Share-based compensation | 145 | 116 | 378 | 639 |
| Restructuring charges | — | — | (11) | (11) |
| Segment Adjusted EBITDA | $(351) | $2,087 | $(609) | $1,127 |

For Q1 2026, the depreciation and amortization for the Connectivity segment was **$783 million**.
